title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction · Structure Brief: Memo Tabulation
summary: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 memo tabulation increment in a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.
Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
